query: xz utils windows native mingw-w64 MSVC command-line injection vulnerability versions affected
a_issues: No significant factual flaws; the tukaani.org and Debian tracker results clearly state affected versions (5.6.2 and older) and fixed version (5.6.3), with mingw-w64/MSVC affected and Cygwin/MSYS2 unaffected. The NVD change history snippet is a bit noisy/hard to parse but still confirms version <5.6.3 affected.
b_issues: No significant factual flaws; the tukaani.org result gives the same core facts (5.6.2 and older affected, mingw-w64/MSVC affected, Cygwin/MSYS2 unaffected, fixed in 5.6.3). The NVD snippet is thin (just a description fragment and advisory link) but not incorrect.
reasoning: Both result sets include the authoritative tukaani.org CVE-2024-47611 page with the exact same key facts: XZ Utils 5.6.2 and older built for native Windows (mingw-w64 or MSVC) are vulnerable, Cygwin/MSYS2 builds and liblzma are unaffected, and the fix is in 5.6.3 (with backports to v5.4/v5.2). Content A additionally includes the Debian security tracker confirming the same version details, while Content B includes a slightly more detailed tukaani.org excerpt (mentioning UCRT vs MSVCRT recommendations). Both correctly distinguish this from the unrelated CVE-2024-3094 backdoor (versions 5.6.0/5.6.1), avoiding confusion. Neither set contains contradictions or factual errors relevant to answering the question, so they are equally capable of enabling a correct answer.
